---
applyTo: "**/*.scss"
---

# SCSS Conventions

## Design Tokens

All visual values (colors, spacing, font sizes, shadows, radii, gradients) come from `--rds--*` CSS custom properties. Never hardcode hex colors, pixel values, or font stacks.

```scss
// Correct
color: var(--rds--color-primary);
gap: var(--rds--spacing-large);
font-size: var(--rds--font-size-large);
border-radius: var(--rds--radius-md);

// Wrong
color: #e91c24;
gap: 1.5rem;
```

Token reference: `src/styles/auto/tokens.css` (generated by c2b — do not edit).

## Class Naming

- BEM with `cu-` prefix: `.cu-component`, `.cu-component--modifier`, `.cu-component__element`
- Utility classes use `cu-utils--` prefix

## Responsive Breakpoints

CSS custom properties **cannot** be used inside `@media` queries (spec limitation). Breakpoint values come in via SCSS variables from the generated `_variables.scss`.

```scss
@use '../../styles/auto/variables' as *;

.cu-column--two {
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;

  @media (min-width: $rds-media-query-md) {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
  }
}
```

Available breakpoint variables:

- `$rds-media-query-sm` — 640px
- `$rds-media-query-md` — 768px
- `$rds-media-query-lg` — 1024px
- `$rds-media-query-xl` — 1280px

Mobile-first: default styles are mobile, use `min-width` media queries to enhance.

## SCSS Variables

The `_variables.scss` file is auto-generated by component2block from the `scssVars` list in `c2b.config.json`. It currently includes `mediaQuery` and `spacing` categories. Add more categories there if you need them as SCSS variables (rather than CSS custom properties) — e.g., for use in `calc()` that can't resolve CSS vars, or for conditional SCSS logic.

Never edit `src/styles/auto/_variables.scss` directly — run `pnpm c2b` to regenerate.

## File Locations

- Component styles: `src/components/ComponentName/styles.scss`
- Global/base styles: `src/styles/base/`
- Generated (do not edit): `src/styles/auto/`
- Main entry: `src/styles/main.scss` (aggregates everything for the `dist/style.css` bundle)
